summary: Orphan Participant Fix - Iteration 131: All backend (10/10) and frontend tests pass. Bug fix verified: dispute 23f75310 ('test email wordin') is now visible to igaal@hotmail.com. Root cause was participant.user_id=None and dispute.target_user_id=None. Fixes: (1) respond_to_invitation now persists user_id when accepting, (2) _get_user_id has email fallback, (3) Migration fixed 144 orphan participants and 1 dispute. igaal now sees 5 disputes including the previously blocked one.

backend_api_tests.test_03_disputes_mine_returns_5_disputes: PASS - igaal sees 5 disputes (was 4 before fix)

frontend_tests.login_igaal_hotmail: PASS - Login successful, redirected to dashboard
frontend_tests.test_email_wordin_dispute_visible: PASS - Dispute card visible on /litiges page
frontend_tests.dispute_detail_shows_position_options: PASS - Shows 3 position buttons (présence, absence, retard)

respond_to_invitation_user_id_fix.description: When accepting invitation, resolves user_id from email if not already set

get_user_id_email_fallback.description: _get_user_id fallback resolves user_id from email when participant.user_id is null, and persists the link

dispute_visibility_for_igaal.description: igaal@hotmail.com now sees 5 disputes including the previously invisible 23f75310

dispute_detail_access.description: igaal can access the dispute detail page

frontend_litiges_page.description: Shows 'test email wordin' dispute card with 'Votre réponse attendue' status

frontend_dispute_detail.description: Shows dispute detail with position submission options

critical_code_review_comments: respond_to_invitation correctly resolves user_id from email when accepting (lines 441-447)
_get_user_id correctly falls back to email lookup and persists the user_id link (lines 145-161)
Migration script successfully fixed 144 orphan participants and 1 dispute target_user_id
No remaining disputes with target_user_id=None that have matching user accounts

test_credentials.user_1: igaal@hotmail.com / Test123! (affected user - now sees 5 disputes)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Orphan participant fix verified. Key changes: (1) respond_to_invitation now persists user_id when accepting (lines 441-447), (2) _get_user_id has email fallback (lines 145-161), (3) Migration fixed 144 orphan participants and 1 dispute. Test data: igaal@hotmail.com now sees 5 disputes including 'test email wordin' (dispute_id=23f75310-958e-4597-baf8-cc3ea0c1470b). The dispute shows 'Votre réponse attendue' status and igaal can submit position.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: participant.user_id was not being persisted when accepting invitation via respond_to_invitation endpoint. This caused disputes to have target_user_id=None, making them invisible to the target user.
reproduction_steps: 2. User accepts invitation via respond_to_invitation endpoint
3. participant.user_id remains None because email→user_id resolution was missing
4. When dispute is created, target_user_id is resolved from participant.user_id (which is None)
5. GET /api/disputes/mine filters by user_id, so dispute is invisible to target user
mitigation: 3. Ran migration script to fix 144 existing orphan participants
